decontamination_notes: Original scenario written for VulcanBench on 2026-06-28. The forms validators, the ValidationError convention, and the validate_username rubric are not derived from any existing repository, so they post-date all model training cutoffs.

blocking: validate_username accepts only 3-20 character usernames of letters, digits, or underscores, and rejects shorter, longer, or otherwise-invalid input.
The change modifies only the forms module and adds no new third-party dependency.

weighted.criterion: Invalid input raises ValidationError (the module's own exception type), not ValueError, an assert, or a returned boolean/None.
On success it returns the validated/cleaned username, consistent with validate_email and validate_age returning their value.
It raises ValidationError with the same (field, message) shape as the sibling validators, using field 'username'.
Signature, naming, and style match the neighboring validators.
